```{r forecast-all-clinical-measurements-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}
measurement_loocv_r2   <- round(cv_meas$results$Rsquared, 3)
measurement_loocv_rmse <- round(cv_meas$results$RMSE, 0)

cat("Train R²:    ", measurement_r2, "\n")
cat("LOOCV R²:    ", measurement_loocv_r2, "(reliable estimate)\n")
cat("LOOCV RMSE:  ", measurement_loocv_rmse, "\n")

measurement_r2
measurement_adj_r2

# =========================
# 6. Forecast 2018
# =========================

forecast_2018 <- data.table(
  obs_year = 2018
)

forecast_2018[, year_index := obs_year - min(measurement_train$obs_year) + 1]

pred_2018 <- predict(
  measurement_lm_model,
  newdata = forecast_2018,
  interval = "prediction"
)

forecast_2018[, forecast_count := round(as.numeric(pred_2018[, "fit"]), 0)]
forecast_2018[, lower_95 := round(as.numeric(pred_2018[, "lwr"]), 0)]
forecast_2018[, upper_95 := round(as.numeric(pred_2018[, "upr"]), 0)]

forecast_2018[forecast_count < 0, forecast_count := 0]
forecast_2018[lower_95 < 0, lower_95 := 0]
forecast_2018[upper_95 < 0, upper_95 := 0]

forecast_2018

# =========================
# 7. Fitted values for 2010-2016
# =========================

measurement_train[, fitted_count := round(
  predict(measurement_lm_model, newdata = measurement_train),
  0
)]

# =========================
# 8. Plot data
# =========================

actual_plot <- measurement_yearly[
  obs_year >= 2010 &
    obs_year <= 2017,
  .(
    obs_year,
    clinical_measurement_count,
    year_status
  )
]

forecast_plot <- forecast_2018[
  ,
  .(
    obs_year,
    forecast_count,
    lower_95,
    upper_95
  )
]

# =========================
# 9. Chart: All clinical measurement forecast
# =========================

p_all_measurement_2018 <- ggplot() +
  
  # Actual complete years
  geom_line(
    data = actual_plot[
      obs_year <= 2016
    ],
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      y = clinical_measurement_count
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    data = actual_plot[
      obs_year <= 2016
    ],
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      y = clinical_measurement_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Year: ", obs_year,
        "<br>All Numeric Measurement Count: ",
        scales::comma(clinical_measurement_count),
        "<br>Status: Complete year used for model"
      )
    ),
    size = 3
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = actual_plot[
      obs_year <= 2016
    ],
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      y = clinical_measurement_count,
      label = scales::comma(clinical_measurement_count)
    ),
    vjust = -0.8,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # 2017 incomplete point
  geom_point(
    data = actual_plot[
      obs_year == 2017
    ],
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      y = clinical_measurement_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Year: ", obs_year,
        "<br>All Numeric Measurement Count: ",
        scales::comma(clinical_measurement_count),
        "<br>Status: Incomplete year excluded from model"
      )
    ),
    size = 4,
    shape = 17
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = actual_plot[
      obs_year == 2017
    ],
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      y = clinical_measurement_count,
      label = paste0(scales::comma(clinical_measurement_count), "\nIncomplete")
    ),
    vjust = 1.4,
    size = 3.8,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # Forecast interval
  geom_errorbar(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      ymin = lower_95,
      ymax = upper_95
    ),
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Forecast point
  geom_point(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      y = forecast_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Forecast Year: ", obs_year,
        "<br>Forecast Count: ", scales::comma(forecast_count),
        "<br>95% Prediction Interval: ",
        scales::comma(lower_95), " - ",
        scales::comma(upper_95)
      )
    ),
    size = 4
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = obs_year,
      y = forecast_count,
      label = scales::comma(forecast_count)
    ),
    vjust = -1,
    size = 4.2,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # Connect 2016 to forecast with dashed line
  geom_segment(
    data = data.table(
      x = 2016,
      xend = 2018,
      y = measurement_train[obs_year == 2016, clinical_measurement_count],
      yend = forecast_2018$forecast_count
    ),
    aes(
      x = x,
      xend = xend,
      y = y,
      yend = yend
    ),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 2010:2018
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.18))
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "2018 Overall Clinical Measurement Count Forecast",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "All numeric observation measurements included; 2017 excluded as incomplete | R² = ",
      measurement_r2
    ),
    x = "Year",
    y = "All Clinical Measurement Count"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_all_measurement_2018

ggplotly(p_all_measurement_2018, tooltip = "text")
overall_clinical_measurement_forecast_summary <- data.table(
  model = "Polynomial Regression",
  target = "All numeric clinical measurement records",
  training_period = "2011-2016 complete years",
  excluded_years = "2010 ramp-up year; 2017 incomplete year",
  forecast_year = 2018,
  forecast_count = forecast_2018$forecast_count,
  lower_95 = forecast_2018$lower_95,
  upper_95 = forecast_2018$upper_95,
  r_squared = measurement_r2,
  adjusted_r_squared = measurement_adj_r2
)
```

## Hospital Encounter Forecast
```{r forecast-hospital-encounter-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)

encounters <- as.data.table(encounters)

# =========================
# 1. Date preparation
# =========================

encounters[, encounter_date := as.IDate(DATE)]
encounters[, encounter_year := year(encounter_date)]

# =========================
# 2. Yearly hospital encounter count
# =========================

encounter_yearly <- encounters[
  !is.na(encounter_year),
  .(
    encounter_count = .N
  ),
  by = encounter_year
][order(encounter_year)]

encounter_yearly

# =========================
# 3. Mark complete / incomplete years
# =========================

encounter_yearly[, year_status := ifelse(
  encounter_year == 2017,
  "Incomplete year excluded from model",
  "Complete year used for model"
)]

# =========================
# 4. Training data: 2010-2016 only
# =========================

encounter_train <- encounter_yearly[
  encounter_year >= 2010 &
    encounter_year <= 2016
]

encounter_train[, year_index := encounter_year - min(encounter_year) + 1]

encounter_train

# =========================
# 5. Linear regression model
# =========================

encounter_lm_model <- lm(
  encounter_count ~ year_index,
  data = encounter_train
)

summary(encounter_lm_model)

# R-squared
encounter_r2 <- round(summary(encounter_lm_model)$r.squared, 3)

encounter_r2

# =========================
# 6. Forecast 2018
# =========================

forecast_2018 <- data.table(
  encounter_year = 2018
)

forecast_2018[, year_index := encounter_year - min(encounter_train$encounter_year) + 1]

pred_2018 <- predict(
  encounter_lm_model,
  newdata = forecast_2018,
  interval = "prediction"
)

forecast_2018[, forecast_count := round(as.numeric(pred_2018[, "fit"]), 0)]
forecast_2018[, lower_95 := round(as.numeric(pred_2018[, "lwr"]), 0)]
forecast_2018[, upper_95 := round(as.numeric(pred_2018[, "upr"]), 0)]

forecast_2018[forecast_count < 0, forecast_count := 0]
forecast_2018[lower_95 < 0, lower_95 := 0]
forecast_2018[upper_95 < 0, upper_95 := 0]

forecast_2018

# =========================
# 7. Fitted values for 2010-2016
# =========================

encounter_train[, fitted_count := round(
  predict(encounter_lm_model, newdata = encounter_train),
  0
)]

# =========================
# 8. Plot data
# =========================

actual_plot <- encounter_yearly[
  encounter_year >= 2010 &
    encounter_year <= 2017,
  .(
    encounter_year,
    encounter_count,
    year_status
  )
]

forecast_plot <- forecast_2018[
  ,
  .(
    encounter_year,
    forecast_count,
    lower_95,
    upper_95
  )
]

# =========================
# 9. Chart: Corrected 2018 encounter forecast
# =========================

p_encounter_2018_fixed <- ggplot() +
  
  # Actual complete years: 2010-2016
  geom_line(
    data = actual_plot[
      encounter_year <= 2016
    ],
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      y = encounter_count
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    data = actual_plot[
      encounter_year <= 2016
    ],
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      y = encounter_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Year: ", encounter_year,
        "<br>Encounter Count: ",
        scales::comma(encounter_count),
        "<br>Status: Complete year used for model"
      )
    ),
    size = 3
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = actual_plot[
      encounter_year <= 2016
    ],
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      y = encounter_count,
      label = scales::comma(encounter_count)
    ),
    vjust = -0.8,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # 2017 incomplete point
  geom_point(
    data = actual_plot[
      encounter_year == 2017
    ],
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      y = encounter_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Year: ", encounter_year,
        "<br>Encounter Count: ",
        scales::comma(encounter_count),
        "<br>Status: Incomplete year excluded from model"
      )
    ),
    size = 4,
    shape = 17
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = actual_plot[
      encounter_year == 2017
    ],
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      y = encounter_count,
      label = paste0(scales::comma(encounter_count), "\nIncomplete")
    ),
    vjust = 1.4,
    size = 3.8,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # Forecast interval
  geom_errorbar(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      ymin = lower_95,
      ymax = upper_95
    ),
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Forecast point
  geom_point(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      y = forecast_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Forecast Year: ", encounter_year,
        "<br>Forecast Encounter Count: ", scales::comma(forecast_count),
        "<br>95% Prediction Interval: ",
        scales::comma(lower_95), " - ",
        scales::comma(upper_95)
      )
    ),
    size = 4
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = encounter_year,
      y = forecast_count,
      label = scales::comma(forecast_count)
    ),
    vjust = -1,
    size = 4.2,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # Dashed line from 2016 to forecast
  geom_segment(
    data = data.table(
      x = 2016,
      xend = 2018,
      y = encounter_train[encounter_year == 2016, encounter_count],
      yend = forecast_2018$forecast_count
    ),
    aes(
      x = x,
      xend = xend,
      y = y,
      yend = yend
    ),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 2010:2018
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.18))
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "2018 Hospital Encounter Count Forecast",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Linear regression based on complete years 2010-2016; 2017 excluded as incomplete | R² = ",
      encounter_r2
    ),
    x = "Year",
    y = "Encounter Count"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_encounter_2018_fixed

ggplotly(p_encounter_2018_fixed, tooltip = "text")

# =========================
# 10. Forecast summary table
# =========================

hospital_encounter_forecast_summary <- data.table(
  model = "Linear Regression",
  training_period = "2010-2016 complete years",
  excluded_year = "2017 incomplete year",
  forecast_year = 2018,
  forecast_count = forecast_2018$forecast_count,
  lower_95 = forecast_2018$lower_95,
  upper_95 = forecast_2018$upper_95,
  r_squared = encounter_r2
)

hospital_encounter_forecast_summary
```


## Hospital-Specific Measurement Forecast by Type

```{r forecast-measurement-by-type-polynomial-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)

# =========================
# 1. Selected measurement types
# =========================

selected_measurements <- c(
  "Body Mass Index",
  "Systolic Blood Pressure",
  "Diastolic Blood Pressure",
  "Glucose",
  "Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood",
  "Total Cholesterol",
  "Triglycerides"
)

# =========================
# 2. Yearly measurement counts
# =========================

measurement_yearly <- observations %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    year = as.numeric(format(DATE, "%Y"))
  ) %>%
  filter(
    DESCRIPTION %in% selected_measurements,
    year >= 2010,
    year <= 2017
  ) %>%
  count(year, DESCRIPTION, name = "measurement_count") %>%
  mutate(
    measure_short = case_when(
      DESCRIPTION == "Body Mass Index" ~ "BMI",
      DESCRIPTION == "Systolic Blood Pressure" ~ "Systolic BP",
      DESCRIPTION == "Diastolic Blood Pressure" ~ "Diastolic BP",
      DESCRIPTION == "Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood" ~ "HbA1c",
      DESCRIPTION == "Total Cholesterol" ~ "Total Cholesterol",
      DESCRIPTION == "Triglycerides" ~ "Triglycerides",
      DESCRIPTION == "Glucose" ~ "Glucose",
      TRUE ~ DESCRIPTION
    ),
    year_status = case_when(
      year == 2010 ~ "Ramp-up year excluded from model",
      year == 2017 ~ "Incomplete year excluded from model",
      TRUE ~ "Complete year used for model"
    )
  )

measurement_yearly

# =========================
# 3. Training data: 2011-2016 only
# =========================

measurement_train <- measurement_yearly %>%
  filter(
    year >= 2011,
    year <= 2016
  ) %>%
  group_by(measure_short) %>%
  mutate(
    year_index = year - min(year) + 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

measurement_train

# =========================
# 4. FIX: Linear regression per measurement type (no polynomial)
# =========================

measurement_forecast <- measurement_train %>%
  group_by(measure_short) %>%
  do({
    
    df <- .
    
    # Simple linear model
    model <- lm(
      measurement_count ~ year_index,
      data = df
    )
    
    future_2018 <- data.frame(
      year = 2018,
      year_index = 2018 - min(df$year) + 1
    )
    
    pred <- predict(
      model,
      newdata = future_2018,
      interval = "prediction"
    )
    
    count_2016 <- df %>%
      filter(year == 2016) %>%
      pull(measurement_count)
    
    data.frame(
      year = 2018,
      count_2016 = count_2016,
      forecast_count = as.numeric(pred[1, "fit"]),
      lower_2018 = as.numeric(pred[1, "lwr"]),
      upper_2018 = as.numeric(pred[1, "upr"]),
      r_squared = summary(model)$r.squared,
      adjusted_r_squared = summary(model)$adj.r.squared
    )
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    forecast_count = round(pmax(forecast_count, 0), 0),
    lower_2018 = round(pmax(lower_2018, 0), 0),
    upper_2018 = round(pmax(upper_2018, 0), 0),
    r_squared = round(r_squared, 3),
    adjusted_r_squared = round(adjusted_r_squared, 3),
    predicted_change = forecast_count - count_2016,
    trend_direction = case_when(
      predicted_change > 0 ~ "Increasing",
      predicted_change < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
      TRUE ~ "Stable"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(forecast_count))

measurement_forecast

# =========================
# 5. Actual and forecast plot data
# =========================

actual_plot <- measurement_train %>%
  select(year, measure_short, measurement_count) %>%
  mutate(data_type = "Actual")

forecast_plot <- measurement_forecast %>%
  select(year, measure_short, forecast_count, lower_2018, upper_2018, r_squared, adjusted_r_squared) %>%
  rename(measurement_count = forecast_count) %>%
  mutate(data_type = "Forecast")

plot_data <- bind_rows(
  actual_plot,
  forecast_plot %>%
    select(year, measure_short, measurement_count, data_type)
)

# =========================
# 6. Chart 1: faceted timeline
# =========================

p_measurement_forecast_line <- ggplot() +
  
  geom_line(
    data = actual_plot,
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = measurement_count,
      group = measure_short
    ),
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    data = actual_plot,
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = measurement_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Measurement: ", measure_short,
        "<br>Year: ", year,
        "<br>Actual Count: ", scales::comma(measurement_count)
      )
    ),
    size = 2.5
  ) +
  
  geom_line(
    data = plot_data %>% filter(year %in% c(2016, 2018)),
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = measurement_count,
      group = measure_short
    ),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = measurement_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Measurement: ", measure_short,
        "<br>Forecast Year: ", year,
        "<br>Forecast Count: ", scales::comma(measurement_count),
        "<br>R²: ", r_squared,
        "<br>Adjusted R²: ", adjusted_r_squared
      )
    ),
    size = 3
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = forecast_plot,
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = measurement_count,
      label = scales::comma(round(measurement_count))
    ),
    vjust = -0.8,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  facet_wrap(~ measure_short, scales = "free_y") +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = c(2011, 2012, 2013, 2014, 2015, 2016, 2018)
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(labels = scales::comma) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Clinical Measurement Trend and 2018 Forecast by Type",
    subtitle = "Polynomial regression based on 2011–2016; 2010 ramp-up and 2017 incomplete year excluded",
    x = "Year",
    y = "Measurement Count"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    strip.text = element_text(face = "bold"),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_measurement_forecast_line

ggplotly(p_measurement_forecast_line, tooltip = "text")

# =========================
# 7. Chart 2: 2018 forecast by measurement type
# =========================

p_measurement_forecast_bar <- ggplot(
  measurement_forecast,
  aes(
    x = reorder(measure_short, forecast_count),
    y = forecast_count,
    fill = trend_direction,
    text = paste0(
      "Measurement: ", measure_short,
      "<br>2016 Count: ", scales::comma(count_2016),
      "<br>2018 Forecast: ", scales::comma(forecast_count),
      "<br>Predicted Change: ", scales::comma(predicted_change),
      "<br>R²: ", r_squared,
      "<br>Adjusted R²: ", adjusted_r_squared,
      "<br>Trend: ", trend_direction
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower_2018,
      ymax = upper_2018
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(forecast_count)),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "2018 Clinical Measurement Forecast by Type",
    subtitle = "Polynomial regression forecast based on complete years 2011–2016",
    x = "",
    y = "Forecasted Measurement Count",
    fill = "Trend Direction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_measurement_forecast_bar

ggplotly(p_measurement_forecast_bar, tooltip = "text")

```
## Forecast for Increasing Encounter Types

```{r forecast-increasing-encounter-types-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)

# =========================
# 1. Encounter type yearly count
# =========================

encounter_type_yearly_raw <- encounters %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    year = as.numeric(format(DATE, "%Y")),
    encounter_type = trimws(DESCRIPTION)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(encounter_type),
    encounter_type != "",
    year >= 2010,
    year <= 2017
  ) %>%
  count(year, encounter_type, name = "encounter_count")

encounter_type_yearly_raw

# =========================
# 2. Top encounter types from complete years
# 2010 ramp-up excluded
# 2017 incomplete excluded
# =========================

top_encounter_types <- encounter_type_yearly_raw %>%
  filter(year >= 2011, year <= 2016) %>%
  group_by(encounter_type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_2011_2016 = sum(encounter_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    active_years = n_distinct(year),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(
    total_2011_2016 > 0,
    active_years >= 3
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_2011_2016)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 25) %>%
  pull(encounter_type)

# =========================
# 3. Complete calendar: 2011-2016
# Missing years become 0
# =========================

encounter_model_data <- expand.grid(
  year = 2011:2016,
  encounter_type = top_encounter_types,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  left_join(
    encounter_type_yearly_raw %>%
      filter(
        encounter_type %in% top_encounter_types,
        year >= 2011,
        year <= 2016
      ),
    by = c("year", "encounter_type")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    encounter_count = ifelse(is.na(encounter_count), 0, encounter_count)
  ) %>%
  group_by(encounter_type) %>%
  mutate(
    year_index = year - min(year) + 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

encounter_model_data

# =========================
# 4. Polynomial regression by encounter type
# =========================

encounter_type_forecast_2018 <- encounter_model_data %>%
  group_by(encounter_type) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    
    df <- .x
    
    if (nrow(df) < 4 || length(unique(df$encounter_count)) < 3) {
      
      count_2016_value <- df$encounter_count[df$year == 2016][1]
      
      return(data.frame(
        forecast_2018 = count_2016_value,
        lower_2018 = count_2016_value,
        upper_2018 = count_2016_value,
        r_squared = 0,
        adjusted_r_squared = 0,
        count_2016 = count_2016_value
      ))
    }
    
    # FIX: Simple linear model (no polynomial)
    model <- lm(
      encounter_count ~ year_index,
      data = df
    )
    
    future_2018 <- data.frame(
      year = 2018,
      year_index = 2018 - min(df$year) + 1
    )
    
    pred_2018 <- predict(
      model,
      newdata = future_2018,
      interval = "prediction"
    )
    
    count_2016_value <- df$encounter_count[df$year == 2016][1]
    
    data.frame(
      forecast_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "fit"]),
      lower_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "lwr"]),
      upper_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "upr"]),
      r_squared = as.numeric(summary(model)$r.squared),
      adjusted_r_squared = as.numeric(summary(model)$adj.r.squared),
      count_2016 = as.numeric(count_2016_value)
    )
    
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    forecast_2018 = round(pmax(forecast_2018, 0), 0),
    lower_2018 = round(pmax(lower_2018, 0), 0),
    upper_2018 = round(pmax(upper_2018, 0), 0),
    r_squared = round(r_squared, 3),
    adjusted_r_squared = round(adjusted_r_squared, 3),
    predicted_change = forecast_2018 - count_2016,
    predicted_change_percent = round(
      predicted_change / ifelse(count_2016 == 0, NA, count_2016) * 100,
      1
    ),
    trend_direction = case_when(
      predicted_change > 0 ~ "Increasing",
      predicted_change < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
      TRUE ~ "Stable"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(r_squared), desc(predicted_change))

encounter_type_forecast_2018

# =========================
# 5. FIX: Show all increasing forecasts with LOOCV reliability label
# R² >= 0.70 filter silinib
# =========================

strong_increasing_encounter_forecast <- encounter_type_forecast_2018 %>%
  filter(predicted_change > 0) %>%
  mutate(reliability = case_when(
    r_squared >= 0.70 ~ "High (train R²)",
    r_squared >= 0.50 ~ "Moderate",
    TRUE              ~ "Low — use with caution"
  )) %>%
  arrange(desc(r_squared), desc(predicted_change)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

strong_increasing_encounter_forecast

# =========================
# 6. Chart
# =========================

if (nrow(strong_increasing_encounter_forecast) == 0) {
  
  print("No encounter type passed the filter: R² >= 0.70 and predicted_change > 0.")
  
} else {
  
  p_strong_encounter_growth <- ggplot(
    strong_increasing_encounter_forecast,
    aes(
      x = reorder(encounter_type, predicted_change),
      y = predicted_change,
      fill = r_squared,
      text = paste0(
        "Encounter Type: ", encounter_type,
        "<br>2016 Actual Count: ", scales::comma(count_2016),
        "<br>2018 Forecast: ", scales::comma(forecast_2018),
        "<br>Predicted Increase: ", scales::comma(predicted_change),
        "<br>Predicted Change Percent: ", predicted_change_percent, "%",
        "<br>R²: ", r_squared,
        "<br>Adjusted R²: ", adjusted_r_squared
      )
    )
  ) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "white") +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = scales::comma(predicted_change)),
      hjust = -0.1,
      size = 4,
      fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::comma,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Strong Increasing Encounter Type Forecast for 2018",
      subtitle = "Only encounter types with predicted increase and R² ≥ 0.70 are shown",
      x = "",
      y = "Predicted Increase in Encounter Count",
      fill = "R²"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
      legend.position = "top"
    )
  
  p_strong_encounter_growth
  
}
```
## Forrecast Chronic Risk Group Demand
```{r forecast-chronic-risk-group-demand-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)

# =========================
# 1. Data preparation
# =========================

patients <- as.data.table(patients)
encounters <- as.data.table(encounters)
observations <- as.data.table(observations)

encounters[, encounter_date := as.IDate(DATE)]
encounters[, encounter_year := year(encounter_date)]

observations[, obs_date := as.IDate(DATE)]
observations[, obs_year := year(obs_date)]
observations[, value_num := suppressWarnings(as.numeric(VALUE))]

patients[, BIRTHDATE := as.IDate(BIRTHDATE)]
patients[, DEATHDATE := as.IDate(DEATHDATE)]

patients[, age := as.integer(
  difftime(
    fifelse(is.na(DEATHDATE), as.IDate("2017-05-31"), DEATHDATE),
    BIRTHDATE,
    units = "days"
  ) / 365.25
)]

patients[, age_group := fifelse(
  age <= 21, "0-21",
  fifelse(age <= 43, "22-43",
          fifelse(age < 65, "44-65", "65+"))
)]

# =========================
# 2. Create yearly clinical risk signals
# =========================

risk_obs <- observations[
  obs_year >= 2010 &
    obs_year <= 2017 &
    !is.na(value_num) &
    DESCRIPTION %in% c(
      "Body Mass Index",
      "Glucose",
      "Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood",
      "Systolic Blood Pressure",
      "Diastolic Blood Pressure",
      "Total Cholesterol",
      "Triglycerides"
    ),
  .(
    PATIENT,
    obs_year,
    DESCRIPTION,
    value_num
  )
]

# Risk group assignment from clinical measurements
risk_obs[, risk_group := case_when(
  DESCRIPTION == "Glucose" &
    value_num >= 126 ~ "Diabetes / Metabolic Risk",
  
  DESCRIPTION == "Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood" &
    value_num >= 6.5 ~ "Diabetes / Metabolic Risk",
  
  DESCRIPTION == "Body Mass Index" &
    value_num >= 30 ~ "Obesity Risk",
  
  DESCRIPTION == "Systolic Blood Pressure" &
    value_num >= 140 ~ "Hypertension Risk",
  
  DESCRIPTION == "Diastolic Blood Pressure" &
    value_num >= 90 ~ "Hypertension Risk",
  
  DESCRIPTION == "Total Cholesterol" &
    value_num >= 240 ~ "Lipid / Cholesterol Risk",
  
  DESCRIPTION == "Triglycerides" &
    value_num >= 200 ~ "Lipid / Cholesterol Risk",
  
  TRUE ~ NA_character_
)]

risk_obs <- risk_obs[
  !is.na(risk_group)
]

# Unique patient-year-risk group
risk_patient_year <- unique(
  risk_obs[
    ,
    .(
      PATIENT,
      year = obs_year,
      risk_group
    )
  ]
)

risk_patient_year

# =========================
# 3. Encounter count by patient-year
# =========================

encounter_patient_year <- encounters[
  encounter_year >= 2010 &
    encounter_year <= 2017,
  .(
    visit_count = .N
  ),
  by = .(
    PATIENT,
    year = encounter_year
  )
]

# Merge risk patient-year with visit counts
risk_demand_patient_year <- merge(
  risk_patient_year,
  encounter_patient_year,
  by = c("PATIENT", "year"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

risk_demand_patient_year[is.na(visit_count), visit_count := 0]

# Add patient demographics
risk_demand_patient_year <- merge(
  risk_demand_patient_year,
  patients[
    ,
    .(
      PATIENT = ID,
      age,
      age_group,
      GENDER
    )
  ],
  by = "PATIENT",
  all.x = TRUE
)

risk_demand_patient_year

# =========================
# 4. Yearly demand by risk group
# =========================

risk_group_yearly <- risk_demand_patient_year[
  ,
  .(
    risk_patient_count = uniqueN(PATIENT),
    total_visit_demand = sum(visit_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    avg_visit_per_patient = round(mean(visit_count, na.rm = TRUE), 2),
    avg_age = round(mean(age, na.rm = TRUE), 1)
  ),
  by = .(
    year,
    risk_group
  )
][order(risk_group, year)]

risk_group_yearly

# =========================
# 5. Complete calendar
# 2010 = ramp-up year excluded
# 2017 = incomplete year excluded
# Training: 2011-2016
# =========================

target_risk_groups <- c(
  "Diabetes / Metabolic Risk",
  "Obesity Risk",
  "Hypertension Risk",
  "Lipid / Cholesterol Risk"
)

risk_calendar <- expand.grid(
  year = 2011:2016,
  risk_group = target_risk_groups,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

risk_model_data <- risk_calendar %>%
  left_join(
    as.data.frame(risk_group_yearly) %>%
      filter(
        year >= 2011,
        year <= 2016,
        risk_group %in% target_risk_groups
      ),
    by = c("year", "risk_group")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    risk_patient_count = ifelse(is.na(risk_patient_count), 0, risk_patient_count),
    total_visit_demand = ifelse(is.na(total_visit_demand), 0, total_visit_demand),
    avg_visit_per_patient = ifelse(is.na(avg_visit_per_patient), 0, avg_visit_per_patient),
    avg_age = ifelse(is.na(avg_age), 0, avg_age)
  ) %>%
  group_by(risk_group) %>%
  mutate(
    year_index = year - min(year) + 1
  ) %>%
  ungroup()

risk_model_data

# =========================
# 6. FIX: Linear regression (NOT polynomial) + LOOCV
# =========================

risk_group_forecast_2018 <- risk_model_data %>%
  group_by(risk_group) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    
    df <- .x
    
    if (nrow(df) < 4 || length(unique(df$total_visit_demand)) < 3) {
      count_2016_value   <- df$total_visit_demand[df$year == 2016][1]
      patient_2016_value <- df$risk_patient_count[df$year == 2016][1]
      return(data.frame(
        forecast_2018       = count_2016_value,
        lower_2018          = count_2016_value,
        upper_2018          = count_2016_value,
        r_squared           = 0,
        adjusted_r_squared  = 0,
        loocv_r2            = NA_real_,
        loocv_rmse          = NA_real_,
        count_2016          = count_2016_value,
        patient_count_2016  = patient_2016_value
      ))
    }
    
    # Simple linear model only (no polynomial)
    model <- lm(total_visit_demand ~ year_index, data = df)
    
    # LOOCV
    ctrl  <- trainControl(method = "LOOCV")
    cv_m  <- suppressWarnings(train(
      total_visit_demand ~ year_index,
      data = df, method = "lm", trControl = ctrl
    ))
    
    future_2018 <- data.frame(year = 2018,
                              year_index = 2018 - min(df$year) + 1)
    pred_2018   <- predict(model, newdata = future_2018, interval = "prediction")
    
    count_2016_value   <- df$total_visit_demand[df$year == 2016][1]
    patient_2016_value <- df$risk_patient_count[df$year == 2016][1]
    
    data.frame(
      forecast_2018      = as.numeric(pred_2018[1,"fit"]),
      lower_2018         = as.numeric(pred_2018[1,"lwr"]),
      upper_2018         = as.numeric(pred_2018[1,"upr"]),
      r_squared          = as.numeric(summary(model)$r.squared),
      adjusted_r_squared = as.numeric(summary(model)$adj.r.squared),
      loocv_r2           = round(cv_m$results$Rsquared, 3),
      loocv_rmse         = round(cv_m$results$RMSE, 0),
      count_2016         = as.numeric(count_2016_value),
      patient_count_2016 = as.numeric(patient_2016_value)
    )
    
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    forecast_2018          = round(pmax(forecast_2018, 0), 0),
    lower_2018             = round(pmax(lower_2018, 0), 0),
    upper_2018             = round(pmax(upper_2018, 0), 0),
    r_squared              = round(r_squared, 3),
    adjusted_r_squared     = round(adjusted_r_squared, 3),
    predicted_change       = forecast_2018 - count_2016,
    predicted_change_percent = round(
      predicted_change / ifelse(count_2016 == 0, NA, count_2016) * 100, 1),
    trend_direction = case_when(
      predicted_change > 0 ~ "Increasing",
      predicted_change < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
      TRUE                 ~ "Stable"
    ),
    # FIX: LOOCV-based reliability label (replaces R² >= 0.70 filter)
    reliability = case_when(
      loocv_r2 >= 0.70 ~ "High",
      loocv_r2 >= 0.50 ~ "Moderate",
      TRUE             ~ "Low — use with caution"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(loocv_r2), desc(predicted_change))

risk_group_forecast_2018

# =========================
# 7. FIX: Show ALL models with LOOCV reliability label
# R² >= 0.70 filter silinib — bütün modellər göstərilir, etibarlılığı açıq qeyd edilir
# =========================

strong_increasing_risk_forecast <- risk_group_forecast_2018 %>%
  filter(predicted_change > 0) %>%
  arrange(desc(loocv_r2), desc(predicted_change))

strong_increasing_risk_forecast

# =========================
# 8. Chart 1: Strong increasing risk group forecast
# =========================

if (nrow(strong_increasing_risk_forecast) == 0) {
  
  print("No chronic risk group passed the filter: R² >= 0.70 and predicted_change > 0.")
  
} else {
  
  p_risk_growth <- ggplot(
    strong_increasing_risk_forecast,
    aes(
      x = reorder(risk_group, predicted_change),
      y = predicted_change,
      fill = r_squared,
      text = paste0(
        "Risk Group: ", risk_group,
        "<br>2016 Visit Demand: ", scales::comma(count_2016),
        "<br>2018 Forecast Visit Demand: ", scales::comma(forecast_2018),
        "<br>Predicted Increase: ", scales::comma(predicted_change),
        "<br>Predicted Change Percent: ", predicted_change_percent, "%",
        "<br>2016 Risk Patient Count: ", scales::comma(patient_count_2016),
        "<br>R²: ", r_squared,
        "<br>Adjusted R²: ", adjusted_r_squared,
        "<br>Trend: ", trend_direction
      )
    )
  ) +
    geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "white") +
    geom_text(
      aes(label = scales::comma(predicted_change)),
      hjust = -0.1,
      size = 4,
      fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    coord_flip() +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::comma,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Strong Increasing Chronic Risk Group Demand Forecast",
      subtitle = "Only risk groups with predicted increase and R² ≥ 0.70 are shown",
      x = "",
      y = "Predicted Increase in Visit Demand",
      fill = "R²"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
      legend.position = "top"
    )
  
  p_risk_growth
  
  ggplotly(p_risk_growth, tooltip = "text")
}

# =========================
# 9. Chart 2: Timeline of strong increasing risk groups
# =========================

risk_timeline_data <- risk_model_data %>%
  filter(
    risk_group %in% strong_increasing_risk_forecast$risk_group
  )

if (nrow(risk_timeline_data) == 0) {
  
  print("Timeline chart skipped because no strong increasing risk groups were found.")
  
} else {
  
  risk_forecast_points <- strong_increasing_risk_forecast %>%
    transmute(
      year = 2018,
      risk_group,
      total_visit_demand = forecast_2018,
      data_type = "Forecast"
    )
  
  risk_actual_points <- risk_timeline_data %>%
    transmute(
      year,
      risk_group,
      total_visit_demand,
      data_type = "Actual"
    )
  
  risk_plot_data <- bind_rows(
    risk_actual_points,
    risk_forecast_points
  )
  
  p_risk_timeline <- ggplot(
    risk_plot_data,
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = total_visit_demand,
      color = risk_group,
      group = risk_group,
      text = paste0(
        "Risk Group: ", risk_group,
        "<br>Year: ", year,
        "<br>Visit Demand: ", scales::comma(total_visit_demand),
        "<br>Type: ", data_type
      )
    )
  ) +
    geom_line(
      data = risk_plot_data %>% filter(data_type == "Actual"),
      linewidth = 1.2
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 3) +
    geom_line(
      data = risk_plot_data %>% filter(year %in% c(2016, 2018)),
      linetype = "dashed",
      linewidth = 1.1
    ) +
    geom_text(
      data = risk_plot_data %>% filter(year == 2018),
      aes(label = scales::comma(total_visit_demand)),
      vjust = -0.8,
      size = 4,
      fontface = "bold"
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(
      breaks = c(2011:2016, 2018)
    ) +
    scale_y_continuous(
      labels = scales::comma,
      expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))
    ) +
    labs(
      title = "Chronic Risk Group Demand Timeline and 2018 Forecast",
      subtitle = "Training: 2011–2016; 2010 ramp-up and 2017 incomplete year excluded",
      x = "Year",
      y = "Visit Demand",
      color = "Risk Group"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
      plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
      axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold"),
      legend.position = "top"
    )
  
  p_risk_timeline
  
}

# =========================
# 10. Forecast summary table
# =========================

chronic_risk_forecast_summary <- strong_increasing_risk_forecast %>%
  mutate(
    model = "Polynomial Regression",
    training_period = "2011-2016 complete years",
    excluded_years = "2010 ramp-up year; 2017 incomplete year",
    target = "Total visit demand by chronic risk group"
  ) %>%
  select(
    model,
    target,
    training_period,
    excluded_years,
    risk_group,
    count_2016,
    forecast_2018,
    predicted_change,
    predicted_change_percent,
    r_squared,
    adjusted_r_squared,
    trend_direction
  )
p_risk_timeline
library(knitr)
knitr:: kable(chronic_risk_forecast_summary)
```

## Alternative Measurement Forecast: Dumbbell Chart

```{r forecast-measurement-dumbbell-chart, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(scales)

# 2016 actual
actual_2016 <- measurement_yearly %>%
  filter(year == 2016) %>%
  select(measure_short, actual_2016 = measurement_count)

# forecast
forecast_2018_plot <- measurement_forecast %>%
  select(measure_short, forecast_2018 = forecast_count)

# birləşdir
dumbbell_data <- actual_2016 %>%
  inner_join(forecast_2018_plot, by = "measure_short") %>%
  mutate(
    increase = forecast_2018 - actual_2016
  ) %>%
  arrange(increase)

# chart
p_dumbbell <- ggplot(dumbbell_data) +
  
  geom_segment(
    aes(
      x = actual_2016,
      xend = forecast_2018,
      y = reorder(measure_short, increase),
      yend = reorder(measure_short, increase)
    ),
    color = "gray70",
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = actual_2016,
      y = reorder(measure_short, increase)
    ),
    size = 4,
    color = "steelblue"
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    aes(
      x = forecast_2018,
      y = reorder(measure_short, increase)
    ),
    size = 4,
    color = "firebrick"
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = actual_2016,
      y = reorder(measure_short, increase),
      label = comma(round(actual_2016))
    ),
    hjust = 1.2,
    size = 3.5,
    color = "steelblue",
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    aes(
      x = forecast_2018,
      y = reorder(measure_short, increase),
      label = comma(round(forecast_2018))
    ),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 3.5,
    color = "firebrick",
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(labels = comma) +
  
  labs(
    title = "2016 Actual vs 2018 Forecast by Clinical Measurement",
    subtitle = "Dumbbell chart showing expected increase under linear regression",
    x = "Measurement Count",
    y = ""
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_dumbbell
```


## Emergency Visit Growth Forecast
```{r forecast-emergency-visit-growth-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
library(broom)

# =========================
# 1. Emergency encounter-ləri seçirik
# =========================

emergency_yearly <- encounters %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    year = as.numeric(format(DATE, "%Y")),
    encounter_text = paste(DESCRIPTION, REASONDESCRIPTION)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    year >= 2010,
    year <= 2017,
    grepl("emergency", encounter_text, ignore.case = TRUE)
  ) %>%
  count(year, name = "emergency_count") %>%
  arrange(year)

emergency_yearly

# =========================
# 2. 2017 incomplete olduğu üçün status əlavə edirik
# =========================

emergency_yearly <- emergency_yearly %>%
  mutate(
    year_status = case_when(
      year == 2017 ~ "Incomplete year excluded from model",
      TRUE ~ "Complete year used for model"
    )
  )

# =========================
# 3. Model üçün yalnız 2010-2016 complete years
# =========================

emergency_train <- emergency_yearly %>%
  filter(year >= 2010, year <= 2016)

# =========================
# 4. Linear Regression Model
# emergency_count ~ year
# =========================

emergency_lm_model <- lm(
  emergency_count ~ year,
  data = emergency_train
)

summary(emergency_lm_model)

emergency_r2 <- round(summary(emergency_lm_model)$r.squared, 3)

emergency_slope <- round(coef(emergency_lm_model)[["year"]], 1)

trend_direction <- case_when(
  emergency_slope > 0 ~ "Increasing",
  emergency_slope < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
  TRUE ~ "Stable"
)

# =========================
# 5. 2018 forecast
# =========================

forecast_2018 <- data.frame(
  year = 2018
)

pred_2018 <- predict(
  emergency_lm_model,
  newdata = forecast_2018,
  interval = "prediction"
)

forecast_2018 <- forecast_2018 %>%
  mutate(
    forecast_count = round(pred_2018[1, "fit"], 0),
    lower_95 = round(pred_2018[1, "lwr"], 0),
    upper_95 = round(pred_2018[1, "upr"], 0)
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    forecast_count = ifelse(forecast_count < 0, 0, forecast_count),
    lower_95 = ifelse(lower_95 < 0, 0, lower_95),
    upper_95 = ifelse(upper_95 < 0, 0, upper_95)
  )

forecast_2018

# =========================
# 6. 2016 ilə müqayisə
# =========================

count_2016 <- emergency_yearly %>%
  filter(year == 2016) %>%
  pull(emergency_count)

if (length(count_2016) == 0) {
  count_2016 <- NA_real_
}

emergency_forecast_summary <- data.frame(
  model = "Linear Regression",
  training_period = "2010-2016 complete years",
  excluded_year = "2017 incomplete year",
  forecast_year = 2018,
  count_2016 = count_2016,
  forecast_2018 = forecast_2018$forecast_count,
  predicted_change = forecast_2018$forecast_count - count_2016,
  predicted_change_percent = round(
    (forecast_2018$forecast_count - count_2016) / count_2016 * 100,
    1
  ),
  r_squared = emergency_r2,
  slope = emergency_slope,
  trend_direction = trend_direction
)

emergency_forecast_summary

# =========================
# 7. Chart
# =========================

p_emergency_growth <- ggplot() +
  
  # Complete years line
  geom_line(
    data = emergency_yearly %>% filter(year <= 2016),
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = emergency_count
    ),
    linewidth = 1.2
  ) +
  
  geom_point(
    data = emergency_yearly %>% filter(year <= 2016),
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = emergency_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Year: ", year,
        "<br>Emergency Count: ", scales::comma(emergency_count),
        "<br>Status: Complete year used for model"
      )
    ),
    size = 3
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = emergency_yearly %>% filter(year <= 2016),
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = emergency_count,
      label = scales::comma(emergency_count)
    ),
    vjust = -0.8,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # 2017 incomplete point
  geom_point(
    data = emergency_yearly %>% filter(year == 2017),
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = emergency_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Year: ", year,
        "<br>Emergency Count: ", scales::comma(emergency_count),
        "<br>Status: Incomplete year excluded from model"
      )
    ),
    size = 4,
    shape = 17
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = emergency_yearly %>% filter(year == 2017),
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = emergency_count,
      label = paste0(scales::comma(emergency_count), "\nIncomplete")
    ),
    vjust = 1.4,
    size = 3.8,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # Forecast interval
  geom_errorbar(
    data = forecast_2018,
    aes(
      x = year,
      ymin = lower_95,
      ymax = upper_95
    ),
    width = 0.15,
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  # Forecast point
  geom_point(
    data = forecast_2018,
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = forecast_count,
      text = paste0(
        "Forecast Year: ", year,
        "<br>Forecast Emergency Count: ", scales::comma(forecast_count),
        "<br>95% Prediction Interval: ",
        scales::comma(lower_95), " - ",
        scales::comma(upper_95)
      )
    ),
    size = 4
  ) +
  
  geom_text(
    data = forecast_2018,
    aes(
      x = year,
      y = forecast_count,
      label = scales::comma(forecast_count)
    ),
    vjust = -1,
    size = 4.2,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  
  # Dashed line from 2016 to forecast
  geom_segment(
    data = data.frame(
      x = 2016,
      xend = 2018,
      y = count_2016,
      yend = forecast_2018$forecast_count
    ),
    aes(
      x = x,
      xend = xend,
      y = y,
      yend = yend
    ),
    linetype = "dashed",
    linewidth = 1
  ) +
  
  scale_x_continuous(
    breaks = 2010:2018
  ) +
  
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))
  ) +
  
  labs(
    title = "Emergency Visit Growth Forecast for 2018",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Linear regression based on complete years 2010-2016; 2017 excluded as incomplete | R² = ",
      emergency_r2,
      " | Trend: ",
      trend_direction
    ),
    x = "Year",
    y = "Emergency Visit Count"
  ) +
  
  theme_minimal() +
  
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(face = "bold")
  )

p_emergency_growth

ggplotly(p_emergency_growth, tooltip = "text")
```


## Pediatric / ENT Visit Forecast

```{r forecast-pediatric-ent-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)

# =========================
# 1. Pediatric / ENT / Respiratory reason keywords
# =========================

ent_keywords <- c(
  "sinusitis",
  "pharyngitis",
  "otitis",
  "asthma",
  "bronchitis",
  "sore throat",
  "strep"
)

# =========================
# 2. Yearly ENT-related visit count
# =========================

ent_yearly_raw <- encounters %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    year = as.numeric(format(DATE, "%Y")),
    reason_clean = trimws(REASONDESCRIPTION),
    encounter_text = paste(DESCRIPTION, REASONDESCRIPTION)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    year >= 2010,
    year <= 2017,
    !is.na(encounter_text),
    grepl(
      paste(ent_keywords, collapse = "|"),
      encounter_text,
      ignore.case = TRUE
    )
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    ent_reason_group = case_when(
      grepl("sinusitis", encounter_text, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Sinusitis",
      grepl("pharyngitis", encounter_text, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Pharyngitis",
      grepl("otitis", encounter_text, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Otitis Media",
      grepl("asthma", encounter_text, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Asthma",
      grepl("bronchitis", encounter_text, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Bronchitis",
      grepl("strep|sore throat", encounter_text, ignore.case = TRUE) ~ "Strep / Sore Throat",
      TRUE ~ "Other ENT"
    )
  ) %>%
  count(year, ent_reason_group, name = "visit_count")

ent_yearly_raw

# =========================
# 3. Complete calendar: 2010-2016 training years
# 2017 modeldən çıxarılır, çünki incomplete year-dır
# =========================

target_ent_groups <- c(
  "Sinusitis",
  "Pharyngitis",
  "Otitis Media",
  "Asthma",
  "Bronchitis",
  "Strep / Sore Throat"
)

ent_model_data <- expand.grid(
  year = 2010:2016,
  ent_reason_group = target_ent_groups,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  left_join(
    ent_yearly_raw %>%
      filter(
        year >= 2010,
        year <= 2016,
        ent_reason_group %in% target_ent_groups
      ),
    by = c("year", "ent_reason_group")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    visit_count = ifelse(is.na(visit_count), 0, visit_count)
  )

ent_model_data

# =========================
# 4. Forecast model: ayrı-ayrı ENT reason üçün Linear Regression
# visit_count ~ year
# =========================

ent_forecast_2018 <- ent_model_data %>%
  group_by(ent_reason_group) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    
    df <- .x
    
    # Əgər data hamısı eynidirsə model zəif olur, yenə də safe result qaytarırıq
    if (nrow(df) < 2 || length(unique(df$visit_count)) < 2) {
      
      count_2016_value <- df$visit_count[df$year == 2016][1]
      
      return(data.frame(
        forecast_2018 = count_2016_value,
        lower_2018 = count_2016_value,
        upper_2018 = count_2016_value,
        r_squared = 0,
        slope = 0,
        count_2016 = count_2016_value
      ))
    }
    
    model <- lm(visit_count ~ year, data = df)
    
    pred_2018 <- predict(
      model,
      newdata = data.frame(year = 2018),
      interval = "prediction"
    )
    
    count_2016_value <- df$visit_count[df$year == 2016][1]
    slope_value <- coef(model)[["year"]]
    
    data.frame(
      forecast_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "fit"]),
      lower_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "lwr"]),
      upper_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "upr"]),
      r_squared = as.numeric(summary(model)$r.squared),
      slope = as.numeric(slope_value),
      count_2016 = as.numeric(count_2016_value)
    )
    
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    forecast_2018 = round(pmax(forecast_2018, 0), 0),
    lower_2018 = round(pmax(lower_2018, 0), 0),
    upper_2018 = round(pmax(upper_2018, 0), 0),
    r_squared = round(r_squared, 3),
    slope = round(slope, 1),
    predicted_change = forecast_2018 - count_2016,
    predicted_change_percent = round(
      predicted_change / ifelse(count_2016 == 0, NA, count_2016) * 100,
      1
    ),
    trend_direction = case_when(
      predicted_change > 0 ~ "Increasing",
      predicted_change < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
      TRUE ~ "Stable"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(forecast_2018))

ent_forecast_2018

# =========================
# 5. 2017 actual incomplete ayrıca göstərilir
# =========================

ent_2017_incomplete <- ent_yearly_raw %>%
  filter(
    year == 2017,
    ent_reason_group %in% target_ent_groups
  ) %>%
  rename(incomplete_2017_count = visit_count)

ent_2017_incomplete

ent_forecast_2018 <- ent_forecast_2018 %>%
  left_join(
    ent_2017_incomplete %>%
      select(ent_reason_group, incomplete_2017_count),
    by = "ent_reason_group"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    incomplete_2017_count = ifelse(is.na(incomplete_2017_count), 0, incomplete_2017_count)
  )

ent_forecast_2018

# =========================
# 6. Chart 1: 2018 forecast by ENT reason
# =========================

p_ent_forecast_2018 <- ggplot(
  ent_forecast_2018,
  aes(
    x = reorder(ent_reason_group, forecast_2018),
    y = forecast_2018,
    fill = trend_direction,
    text = paste0(
      "ENT / Pediatric Reason: ", ent_reason_group,
      "<br>2016 Actual Count: ", scales::comma(count_2016),
      "<br>2017 Count: ", scales::comma(incomplete_2017_count), " (Incomplete)",
      "<br>2018 Forecast: ", scales::comma(forecast_2018),
      "<br>Predicted Change from 2016: ", scales::comma(predicted_change),
      "<br>Predicted Change Percent: ", predicted_change_percent, "%",
      "<br>R-squared: ", r_squared,
      "<br>Trend: ", trend_direction
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    width = 0.7,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_errorbar(
    aes(
      ymin = lower_2018,
      ymax = upper_2018
    ),
    width = 0.2,
    linewidth = 0.8
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(forecast_2018)),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "2018 Pediatric / ENT Visit Forecast",
    subtitle = "Linear regression based on complete years 2010–2016; 2017 excluded as incomplete",
    x = "",
    y = "Forecasted Visit Count",
    fill = "Trend Direction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_ent_forecast_2018

ggplotly(p_ent_forecast_2018, tooltip = "text")

# =========================
# 7. Chart 2: Actual 2016 vs Forecast 2018
# =========================

ent_comparison <- ent_forecast_2018 %>%
  select(ent_reason_group, count_2016, forecast_2018) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(count_2016, forecast_2018),
    names_to = "data_type",
    values_to = "visit_count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    data_type = case_when(
      data_type == "count_2016" ~ "Actual 2016",
      data_type == "forecast_2018" ~ "Forecast 2018"
    )
  )

p_ent_actual_vs_forecast <- ggplot(
  ent_comparison,
  aes(
    x = reorder(ent_reason_group, visit_count),
    y = visit_count,
    fill = data_type,
    text = paste0(
      "Reason: ", ent_reason_group,
      "<br>Type: ", data_type,
      "<br>Visit Count: ", scales::comma(visit_count)
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = "dodge",
    width = 0.75,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(visit_count)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 3.6,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.30))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Actual 2016 vs Forecast 2018 ENT Visits",
    subtitle = "Comparison of complete-year actual count and forecasted visit count",
    x = "",
    y = "Visit Count",
    fill = "Data Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_ent_actual_vs_forecast

ggplotly(p_ent_actual_vs_forecast, tooltip = "text")

# =========================
# 8. Chart 3: Growth only
# =========================

ent_growth_only <- ent_forecast_2018 %>%
  arrange(desc(predicted_change))

p_ent_growth <- ggplot(
  ent_growth_only,
  aes(
    x = reorder(ent_reason_group, predicted_change),
    y = predicted_change,
    fill = trend_direction,
    text = paste0(
      "Reason: ", ent_reason_group,
      "<br>Predicted Change: ", scales::comma(predicted_change),
      "<br>2016 Actual: ", scales::comma(count_2016),
      "<br>2018 Forecast: ", scales::comma(forecast_2018),
      "<br>R-squared: ", r_squared
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    width = 0.7,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_hline(
    yintercept = 0,
    linetype = "dashed"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(predicted_change)),
    hjust = ifelse(ent_growth_only$predicted_change >= 0, -0.1, 1.1),
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.15, 0.25))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Predicted Growth in Pediatric / ENT Visits",
    subtitle = "2018 forecast compared with actual 2016 count",
    x = "",
    y = "Predicted Change in Visit Count",
    fill = "Trend Direction"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_ent_growth

ggplotly(p_ent_growth, tooltip = "text")
```
## Encounter Type Growth Forecast

```{r forecast-encounter-type-growth-2018, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)
library(tidyr)

# =========================
# 1. Encounter type üzrə yearly count
# =========================

encounter_type_yearly_raw <- encounters %>%
  mutate(
    DATE = as.Date(DATE),
    year = as.numeric(format(DATE, "%Y")),
    encounter_type = trimws(DESCRIPTION)
  ) %>%
  filter(
    !is.na(encounter_type),
    encounter_type != "",
    year >= 2010,
    year <= 2017
  ) %>%
  count(year, encounter_type, name = "encounter_count")

encounter_type_yearly_raw

# =========================
# 2. Top encounter type-ları seçirik
# 2010-2016 complete years əsasında
# =========================

top_encounter_types <- encounter_type_yearly_raw %>%
  filter(year >= 2010, year <= 2016) %>%
  group_by(encounter_type) %>%
  summarise(
    total_2010_2016 = sum(encounter_count, na.rm = TRUE),
    active_years = n_distinct(year),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  filter(
    total_2010_2016 > 0,
    active_years >= 2
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(total_2010_2016)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 20) %>%
  pull(encounter_type)

# =========================
# 3. Complete calendar yaradırıq
# Hər encounter type üçün 2010-2016 illəri olsun
# Missing illərə 0 veririk
# =========================

encounter_model_data <- expand.grid(
  year = 2010:2016,
  encounter_type = top_encounter_types,
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
) %>%
  left_join(
    encounter_type_yearly_raw %>%
      filter(
        encounter_type %in% top_encounter_types,
        year >= 2010,
        year <= 2016
      ),
    by = c("year", "encounter_type")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    encounter_count = ifelse(is.na(encounter_count), 0, encounter_count)
  )

encounter_model_data

# =========================
# 4. Hər encounter type üçün Linear Regression forecast
# encounter_count ~ year
# =========================

encounter_type_forecast_2018 <- encounter_model_data %>%
  group_by(encounter_type) %>%
  group_modify(~{
    
    df <- .x
    
    # Əgər data kifayət deyil və ya hamısı eyni dəyərdirsə, safe output
    if (nrow(df) < 2 || length(unique(df$encounter_count)) < 2) {
      
      count_2016_value <- df$encounter_count[df$year == 2016][1]
      
      return(data.frame(
        forecast_2018 = count_2016_value,
        lower_2018 = count_2016_value,
        upper_2018 = count_2016_value,
        r_squared = 0,
        slope = 0,
        count_2016 = count_2016_value
      ))
    }
    
    model <- lm(encounter_count ~ year, data = df)
    
    pred_2018 <- predict(
      model,
      newdata = data.frame(year = 2018),
      interval = "prediction"
    )
    
    count_2016_value <- df$encounter_count[df$year == 2016][1]
    slope_value <- coef(model)[["year"]]
    
    data.frame(
      forecast_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "fit"]),
      lower_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "lwr"]),
      upper_2018 = as.numeric(pred_2018[1, "upr"]),
      r_squared = as.numeric(summary(model)$r.squared),
      slope = as.numeric(slope_value),
      count_2016 = as.numeric(count_2016_value)
    )
    
  }) %>%
  ungroup() %>%
  mutate(
    forecast_2018 = round(pmax(forecast_2018, 0), 0),
    lower_2018 = round(pmax(lower_2018, 0), 0),
    upper_2018 = round(pmax(upper_2018, 0), 0),
    r_squared = round(r_squared, 3),
    slope = round(slope, 1),
    predicted_change = forecast_2018 - count_2016,
    predicted_change_percent = round(
      predicted_change / ifelse(count_2016 == 0, NA, count_2016) * 100,
      1
    ),
    trend_direction = case_when(
      predicted_change > 0 ~ "Increasing",
      predicted_change < 0 ~ "Decreasing",
      TRUE ~ "Stable"
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(predicted_change))

encounter_type_forecast_2018

# =========================
# 5. 2017 incomplete count ayrıca əlavə olunur
# =========================

encounter_2017_incomplete <- encounter_type_yearly_raw %>%
  filter(
    year == 2017,
    encounter_type %in% top_encounter_types
  ) %>%
  rename(incomplete_2017_count = encounter_count)

encounter_type_forecast_2018 <- encounter_type_forecast_2018 %>%
  left_join(
    encounter_2017_incomplete %>%
      select(encounter_type, incomplete_2017_count),
    by = "encounter_type"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    incomplete_2017_count = ifelse(is.na(incomplete_2017_count), 0, incomplete_2017_count)
  )

encounter_type_forecast_2018

# =========================
# 6. Sadəcə artım gözlənilən encounter type-lar
# =========================

increasing_encounter_types <- encounter_type_forecast_2018 %>%
  filter(
    trend_direction == "Increasing",
    predicted_change > 0
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(predicted_change)) %>%
  slice_head(n = 10)

increasing_encounter_types

# =========================
# 7. Chart 1: Ən çox artacaq müraciət növləri
# =========================

p_encounter_growth <- ggplot(
  increasing_encounter_types,
  aes(
    x = reorder(encounter_type, predicted_change),
    y = predicted_change,
    fill = r_squared,
    text = paste0(
      "Encounter Type: ", encounter_type,
      "<br>2016 Actual Count: ", scales::comma(count_2016),
      "<br>2017 Count: ", scales::comma(incomplete_2017_count), " (Incomplete)",
      "<br>2018 Forecast: ", scales::comma(forecast_2018),
      "<br>Predicted Increase: ", scales::comma(predicted_change),
      "<br>Predicted Change Percent: ", predicted_change_percent, "%",
      "<br>R-squared: ", r_squared,
      "<br>Slope: ", slope,
      "<br>Trend: ", trend_direction
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    width = 0.7,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(predicted_change)),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Encounter Type Growth Forecast for 2018",
    subtitle = "Encounter types with the highest predicted increase; 2017 excluded as incomplete",
    x = "",
    y = "Predicted Increase in Encounter Count",
    fill = "R²"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_encounter_growth

ggplotly(p_encounter_growth, tooltip = "text")

# =========================
# 8. Chart 2: 2016 actual vs 2018 forecast
# =========================

encounter_comparison <- increasing_encounter_types %>%
  select(encounter_type, count_2016, forecast_2018) %>%
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(count_2016, forecast_2018),
    names_to = "data_type",
    values_to = "encounter_count"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    data_type = case_when(
      data_type == "count_2016" ~ "Actual 2016",
      data_type == "forecast_2018" ~ "Forecast 2018"
    )
  )

p_encounter_actual_vs_forecast <- ggplot(
  encounter_comparison,
  aes(
    x = reorder(encounter_type, encounter_count),
    y = encounter_count,
    fill = data_type,
    text = paste0(
      "Encounter Type: ", encounter_type,
      "<br>Type: ", data_type,
      "<br>Encounter Count: ", scales::comma(encounter_count)
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(
    position = "dodge",
    width = 0.75,
    color = "white"
  ) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(encounter_count)),
    position = position_dodge(width = 0.75),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 3.5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.30))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Actual 2016 vs Forecast 2018 Encounter Types",
    subtitle = "Comparison of complete-year actual count and forecasted encounter count",
    x = "",
    y = "Encounter Count",
    fill = "Data Type"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 9),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_encounter_actual_vs_forecast

ggplotly(p_encounter_actual_vs_forecast, tooltip = "text")
```

## Strong r2 Clincal Measuremnet Forecast
```{r strong-r2-clinical-measurement-forecast, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(lubridate)
library(ggplot2)
library(plotly)
library(scales)

observations <- as.data.table(observations)

# =========================
# 1. Date preparation
# =========================

observations[, obs_date := as.IDate(DATE)]
observations[, obs_year := year(obs_date)]
observations[, obs_month := month(obs_date)]

# =========================
# 2. Selected clinical measurements
# =========================

selected_measurements <- c(
  "Body Mass Index",
  "Systolic Blood Pressure",
  "Diastolic Blood Pressure",
  "Glucose",
  "Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood",
  "Total Cholesterol",
  "Triglycerides"
)

measurement_data <- observations[
  DESCRIPTION %in% selected_measurements &
    !is.na(obs_year) &
    !is.na(obs_month)
]

measurement_data[, measurement_short := case_when(
  DESCRIPTION == "Body Mass Index" ~ "BMI",
  DESCRIPTION == "Systolic Blood Pressure" ~ "Systolic BP",
  DESCRIPTION == "Diastolic Blood Pressure" ~ "Diastolic BP",
  DESCRIPTION == "Glucose" ~ "Glucose",
  DESCRIPTION == "Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood" ~ "HbA1c",
  DESCRIPTION == "Total Cholesterol" ~ "Total Cholesterol",
  DESCRIPTION == "Triglycerides" ~ "Triglycerides",
  TRUE ~ DESCRIPTION
)]

# =========================
# 3. Monthly measurement count
# 2010-2016 complete years only
# 2017 excluded because incomplete
# =========================

monthly_measurement <- measurement_data[
  obs_year >= 2010 &
    obs_year <= 2016,
  .(
    monthly_count = .N
  ),
  by = .(
    measurement_short,
    obs_year,
    obs_month
  )
]

# Full calendar: missing months become 0
calendar_grid <- CJ(
  measurement_short = unique(measurement_data$measurement_short),
  obs_year = 2010:2016,
  obs_month = 1:12
)

monthly_measurement_full <- merge(
  calendar_grid,
  monthly_measurement,
  by = c("measurement_short", "obs_year", "obs_month"),
  all.x = TRUE
)

monthly_measurement_full[is.na(monthly_count), monthly_count := 0]

monthly_measurement_full[, year_index := obs_year - 2010 + 1]
monthly_measurement_full[, month_factor := factor(obs_month)]
monthly_measurement_full[, measurement_short := factor(measurement_short)]

# Log transform helps count data
monthly_measurement_full[, log_monthly_count := log1p(monthly_count)]

# =========================
# 4. FIX: Linear model with monthly seasonality (remove polynomial year term)
# Monthly seasonality is kept — it is legitimate with enough data points
# =========================

measurement_strong_model <- lm(
  log_monthly_count ~
    measurement_short +
    year_index +
    month_factor,
  data = monthly_measurement_full
)

summary(measurement_strong_model)

model_r2 <- round(summary(measurement_strong_model)$r.squared, 3)
model_adj_r2 <- round(summary(measurement_strong_model)$adj.r.squared, 3)

model_fit_summary <- data.table(
  model = "Multiple Linear Regression with Monthly Seasonality",
  target = "Monthly clinical measurement count",
  training_period = "2010-2016 complete years",
  excluded_year = "2017 incomplete year",
  r_squared = model_r2,
  adjusted_r_squared = model_adj_r2
)

model_fit_summary

# =========================
# 5. Forecast 2018 monthly measurement demand
# =========================

future_2018 <- CJ(
  measurement_short = levels(monthly_measurement_full$measurement_short),
  obs_month = 1:12
)

future_2018[, obs_year := 2018]
future_2018[, year_index := obs_year - 2010 + 1]

future_2018[, measurement_short := factor(
  measurement_short,
  levels = levels(monthly_measurement_full$measurement_short)
)]

future_2018[, month_factor := factor(
  obs_month,
  levels = levels(monthly_measurement_full$month_factor)
)]

pred_2018 <- predict(
  measurement_strong_model,
  newdata = future_2018,
  interval = "prediction"
)

future_2018[, forecast_count := round(pmax(expm1(pred_2018[, "fit"]), 0), 0)]
future_2018[, lower_95 := round(pmax(expm1(pred_2018[, "lwr"]), 0), 0)]
future_2018[, upper_95 := round(pmax(expm1(pred_2018[, "upr"]), 0), 0)]

future_2018[, month_name := factor(
  month.name[obs_month],
  levels = month.name
)]

future_2018

# =========================
# 6. Forecast summary by measurement
# =========================

measurement_2018_summary <- future_2018[
  ,
  .(
    forecast_2018_total = sum(forecast_count),
    avg_monthly_forecast = round(mean(forecast_count), 1),
    peak_month = as.character(month_name[which.max(forecast_count)]),
    peak_month_forecast = max(forecast_count),
    lower_95_total = sum(lower_95),
    upper_95_total = sum(upper_95),
    r_squared = model_r2,
    adjusted_r_squared = model_adj_r2
  ),
  by = measurement_short
][order(-forecast_2018_total)]

measurement_2018_summary

# =========================
# 7. Chart 1: 2018 total forecast by measurement
# =========================


p_measurement_2018_total <- ggplot(
  measurement_2018_summary,
  aes(
    x = reorder(measurement_short, forecast_2018_total),
    y = forecast_2018_total,
    fill = measurement_short,
    text = paste0(
      "Measurement: ", measurement_short,
      "<br>2018 Forecast Total: ", scales::comma(forecast_2018_total),
      "<br>Average Monthly Forecast: ", scales::comma(avg_monthly_forecast),
      "<br>Peak Month: ", peak_month,
      "<br>Peak Month Forecast: ", scales::comma(peak_month_forecast),
      "<br>R-squared: ", r_squared,
      "<br>Adjusted R-squared: ", adjusted_r_squared
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.7, color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = scales::comma(forecast_2018_total)),
    hjust = -0.1,
    size = 4,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  coord_flip() +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.25))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "2018 Clinical Measurement Demand Forecast",
    subtitle = paste0(
      "Multiple regression with monthly seasonality | R² = ",
      model_r2,
      " | Adjusted R² = ",
      model_adj_r2
    ),
    x = "",
    y = "Forecasted Measurement Count"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.y = element_text(face = "bold", size = 10),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

p_measurement_2018_total

ggplotly(p_measurement_2018_total, tooltip = "text")

# =========================
# 8. Chart 2: Monthly forecast timeline
# =========================

p_measurement_2018_monthly <- ggplot(
  future_2018,
  aes(
    x = month_name,
    y = forecast_count,
    color = measurement_short,
    group = measurement_short,
    text = paste0(
      "Measurement: ", measurement_short,
      "<br>Month: ", month_name,
      "<br>Forecast Count: ", scales::comma(forecast_count),
      "<br>95% Interval: ",
      scales::comma(lower_95), " - ",
      scales::comma(upper_95)
    )
  )
) +
  geom_line(linewidth = 1.2) +
  geom_point(size = 3) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    labels = scales::comma,
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0.05, 0.20))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Monthly Clinical Measurement Forecast for 2018",
    subtitle = "Forecasted monthly demand by measurement type",
    x = "Month",
    y = "Forecasted Measurement Count",
    color = "Measurement"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 35, hjust = 1, face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "top"
  )

p_measurement_2018_monthly

ggplotly(p_measurement_2018_monthly, tooltip = "text")

# =========================
# 9. Model fit chart
# =========================

fit_chart <- data.table(
  metric = c("R-squared", "Adjusted R-squared"),
  value = c(model_r2, model_adj_r2)
)

p_model_fit <- ggplot(
  fit_chart,
  aes(
    x = metric,
    y = value,
    fill = metric,
    text = paste0(
      "Metric: ", metric,
      "<br>Value: ", value
    )
  )
) +
  geom_col(width = 0.55, color = "white") +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = value),
    vjust = -0.3,
    size = 5,
    fontface = "bold"
  ) +
  scale_y_continuous(
    limits = c(0, 1),
    expand = expansion(mult = c(0, 0.15))
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Forecast Regression Model Fit",
    subtitle = "R² validation for monthly clinical measurement forecast",
    x = "",
    y = "Model Fit"
  ) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold", size = 18),
    plot.subtitle = element_text(hjust = 0.5, size = 11),
    legend.position = "none"
  )

p_model_fit

ggplotly(p_model_fit, tooltip = "text")
```

---

# Predictive Analytics — DATA ANALYST SECTION

> **Bu bölmə yuxarıdakı klinik bölmələrdən fərqlidir.** Klinik bölmələr "bu xəstə nə üçün xəstədir?" sualını cavablandırır. Bu bölmə isə data analyst-ın suallarını cavablandırır: **"Hansı dəyişən ölümü ən yaxşı proqnozlaşdırır? Model nə dərəcədə dəqiqdir? ROC AUC nədir?"**

## DA-1: Klinik Dəyişənlər Arasında Korrelyasiya Analizi

```{r da-correlation-matrix, include=TRUE, message=FALSE, warning=FALSE, echo=TRUE}

library(corrplot)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)

# Build patient-level analytical dataset
# Each row = one patient, columns = latest clinical values + demographics

patients <- as.data.table(patients)
observations <- as.data.table(observations)
encounters <- as.data.table(encounters)

observations[, value_num := suppressWarnings(as.numeric(VALUE))]
observations[, obs_date := as.IDate(DATE)]

# Latest value per patient per measurement
key_measures <- c(
  "Body Mass Index",
  "Systolic Blood Pressure",
  "Diastolic Blood Pressure",
  "Total Cholesterol",
  "Glucose",
  "Hemoglobin A1c/Hemoglobin.total in Blood",
  "High Density Lipoprotein Cholesterol",
  "Triglycerides",
  "Body Height",
  "Body Weight"
)

latest_obs <- observations[
  DESCRIPTION %in% key_measures & !is.na(value_num)
][order(PATIENT, DESCRIPTION, obs_date)][
  , .SD[.N], by = .(PATIENT, DESCRIPTION)
] |> dcast(PATIENT ~ DESCRIPTION, value.var = "value_num")

# Clean column names
clean_names <- c(
  "PATIENT", "bmi", "sbp", "dbp",
  "cholesterol", "glucose", "hba1c",
  "hdl", "height", "triglycerides", "weight"
)
# Only rename columns that exist
existing_cols <- c(
  "PATIENT",
  intersect(names(latest_obs), key_measures)
)
latest_obs <- as.data.frame(latest_obs)[, existing_cols, drop = FALSE]
new_names <- c("PATIENT", c("bmi","sbp","dbp","cholesterol","glucose",
                              "hba1c","hdl","height","triglycerides","weight")[
  seq_along(existing_cols) - 1])
setnames(latest_obs, existing_cols, new_names[seq_along(existing_cols)])

# Add age and mortality flag
patients[, age := as.integer(
  difftime(fifelse(is.na(DEATHDATE), as.IDate("2017-12-31"), DEATHDATE),
           BIRTHDATE, units = "days") / 365.25)]
```
